highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.146 Released
highlights.summary: The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review` and now supports an optional effort level (e.g. `/code-review high`), giving you more control over review depth. This release also fixes a Windows PowerShell bug and improves auto mode behavior so `AskUserQuestion` is no longer suppressed when the user or a skill depends on it.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.145 Released
highlights.summary: You can now run `claude agents --json` to list all live Claude sessions as JSON, making it easy to integrate with scripting tools, tmux plugins, or status bars. This release also adds `agent_id` and `parent_agent_id` to OpenTelemetry spans, improving observability for multi-agent workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142: Fast Mode Now Uses Opus 4.7 by Default
highlights.summary: Fast mode has been upgraded to use Claude Opus 4.7 by default, replacing Opus 4.6 for snappier everyday tasks. This update also adds a rich set of new flags to `claude agents` — including `--model`, `--effort`, `--permission-mode`, and more — giving you fine-grained control over how background sessions are dispatched.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest model powering Claude Code's fast mode. If you're building or using Claude Code, this is the model now running under the hood for many of your tasks — worth reading the announcement to understand its capabilities and improvements.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial covers Claude Code from the basics all the way to more advanced usage in just 20 minutes, making it ideal if you want a fast but comprehensive on-ramp. It's a great starting point before diving into the official docs or experimenting on your own projects.

highlights.title: Session Amnesia: The Hidden Cost of Stateless AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: This article explores how stateless AI coding tools lose context between sessions, forcing developers to repeatedly re-explain their codebase and slowing down workflows. Understanding this limitation can help you build better habits — like maintaining a detailed CLAUDE.md file — to preserve context across sessions in Claude Code.

highlights.title: Why Did Karpathy Join Anthropic? (Bilibili: Karpathy为何要加入Anthropic？)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video discusses Andrej Karpathy's decision to join Anthropic and what it might signal about the company's technical direction for Claude. For Claude Code users, Karpathy's focus on developer tooling and AI education could hint at exciting future improvements to the coding experience.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/code-review high` (new in v2.1.146) when you want Claude to perform a thorough, deep review of complex or critical code — save the default effort level for quick day-to-day checks to keep things fast.

Keep a `CLAUDE.md` file at the root of your project with key architecture decisions, coding conventions, and important context. This acts as persistent memory across sessions and dramatically reduces the 'session amnesia' problem where Claude loses track of your codebase.

Run `claude agents --json` and pipe the output to `jq` to quickly inspect all live Claude sessions by name, ID, and status — handy for scripting session pickers or monitoring long-running background agents from your terminal.
